title: Resilient External Integration Pattern
description: Enforces mandatory resilience controls (circuit breaker + retry) on every external service integration. Every call crossing a trust boundary must be protected against cascading failure.

book-references: Cloud Native Patterns Ch. 10 — Retries & Idempotency
Cloud Native Patterns Ch. 11 — Circuit Breakers

nodes.name: Calling Service
nodes.description: Internal service making outbound calls to external APIs. Must protect every external call with resilience annotations.

nodes.name: External API
nodes.description: Third-party system outside our trust boundary. May be slow, flaky, or unavailable.

relationships.description: External call protected by @CircuitBreaker + @Retry with named instances and typed fallback methods

constraints.description: Every method calling an external API must have @CircuitBreaker with a named instance defined in application.yml.
Every method calling an external API must have @Retry with exponential backoff configured in application.yml.
Every @CircuitBreaker must specify a fallbackMethod that returns the same type + accepts Throwable. Fallback must never throw.
Each external service gets its own circuit breaker instance tuned to its latency profile. Never share breakers across unrelated services.
Circuit breaker instances must set registerHealthIndicator=true so /actuator/health reflects breaker state.
Non-idempotent operations (financial debits, irreversible mutations) must not be retried unless idempotency is guaranteed at the provider level.
Idempotency enforced at domain layer (idempotency keys, state machine transitions). Retries at infrastructure layer handle transient failures only.

circuit-breaker-tuning-guide.description: Parameters to tune per external service based on observed latency percentiles

parameters.slidingWindowSize: Number of calls to evaluate. Higher = smoother but slower reaction. Range: 10-50.
parameters.failureRateThreshold: Percentage at which breaker opens. Lower = more sensitive. Range: 30-70.
parameters.waitDurationInOpenState: How long to wait before half-open probe. Longer = more recovery time for slow services. Range: 15s-120s.
parameters.permittedNumberOfCallsInHalfOpenState: Probe calls in half-open state. Higher = more confidence before closing. Range: 3-10.
